data.title: As an AI coding agent, I receive kudos for a high-scoring review, addressing reviewer feedback, and recovering from test gate failures
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Implement review-workflow kudos triggers T3, T14, T15 — high-scoring dimension, addressing reviewer feedback with resubmission, and recovering from test gate failures.
**Why**: These triggers close the feedback loop on the review and test-gate workflow — recognizing agents that respond to and recover from quality gates. They depend only on the ledger infrastructure (Story A) and the existing .review-events/ JSONL and reviewer-findings.json artifacts.
**Scope**:
- IN: T3, T14, T15 trigger implementations in commit-validate.sh; .review-events/ JSONL and reviewer-findings.json parsing
- OUT: sg-based triggers (Story E), AST-based triggers (Story F)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, T3 reads reviewer-findings.json from $ARTIFACTS_DIR; awards kudos when any dimension score=5; one award per dimension per diff_hash; gracefully handles file-absent case (no award, no error); does not modify or delete the file
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-T3
- When this story is complete, T14 detects critical/important finding presence via reviewer-findings.json (critical_count > 0 OR important_count > 0) AND detects resubmission via .review-events/ JSONL commit_workflow event in the same session; awards kudos when both conditions are met
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-T14
- When this story is complete, T15 reads .review-events/ JSONL and awards kudos when a commit_workflow event with blocked_by_test_gate=true is followed by a passing commit in the same session; commits where only changes are .test-index RED marker lines matching pattern ^\[.*\]$ are excluded from the recovery sequence
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-T15
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] T3 and T14 both read reviewer-findings.json — a protected artifact under CLAUDE.md rule 16. Read-only access is permitted (rule 16 prohibits writes/modifications only). Handle file-absent case gracefully. Document these as new consumers in reviewer-findings.json lifecycle notes.
- [Reliability] T14 and T15 JSONL parsing must handle partial/concurrent writes gracefully (partial lines at file end must not crash the parser).

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. High confidence means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
